content.verdict: Anantara Vilamoura brings the Thai hospitality group's signature spa philosophy to one of the Algarve's most active resorts — a marina-side property with multiple pools, an excellent spa, golf access, and easy proximity to Vilamoura's famous beach and casino. It's a polished international resort experience with the Anantara spa as its genuine differentiator; if spa-forward luxury with golf, beach, and marina nightlife on the doorstep is your honeymoon formula, this delivers it consistently well. Couples looking for a raw, romantic, or uniquely Portuguese experience may find it too international and busy.
content.best_room: Book a Deluxe Pool View Suite or a Superior Room with a terrace facing the gardens and pool — the resort's rooms were renovated under the Anantara brand and have a clean, Southeast Asian-influenced luxury aesthetic. Suite guests receive priority spa booking and butler service, which is worthwhile for a honeymoon. Request a room away from the pool area if noise concerns you at night.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Vilamoura Arrival
itinerary_7_nights.description: Arrive at Anantara Vilamoura and check into your room, then walk along the marina promenade — Vilamoura's marina is one of the largest and most attractive in Portugal, lined with yachts, restaurants, and bars. Order a Sagres beer or a glass of local wine at a marina-side restaurant watching the boats come and go. Return to the hotel for the welcome Anantara experience — the scented towels and Thai-inflected service standard is immediately distinct.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Anantara Spa Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Dedicate a full day to the Anantara Spa, which is among the best in the Algarve — a comprehensive Southeast Asian-meets-Portuguese wellness experience with hammam, hydrotherapy pools, and signature treatments including the Douro Valley Ritual (using Portuguese grape seed and essential oils) and the classic Thai couple's massage. The spa also has a relaxation lounge overlooking the gardens.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Falesia Beach Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Take the hotel shuttle to Praia da Falésia — one of the most photogenic beaches in the Algarve, with dramatic 30-metre red and ochre cliff walls running for 6km behind a wide golden beach. It's one of the least-overdeveloped major beaches in the Algarve and the contrast between the cliffs and the turquoise sea is extraordinary. The hotel beach club provides loungers and service.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Faro & the Ria Formosa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 30 minutes to Faro — the Algarve's capital is often overlooked by tourists but has a beautifully preserved walled old town, a cathedral with a tower offering panoramic views, and the remarkable Igreja dos Ossos (a chapel decorated with the bones of clergy). From Faro, take a boat into the Ria Formosa Natural Park — a lagoon system of islands, birds, and shellfish beds that supplies most of Portugal's seafood.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Golf & Sunset Dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: Vilamoura has five world-class golf courses; the Old Course is the most famous — a classic parkland layout that has hosted the Portuguese Open. Book a tee time through the hotel concierge (non-golfers can visit the clubhouse for lunch). In the evening, book the Anantara's signature restaurant Boa Mesa for a tasting menu of contemporary Portuguese cuisine that is the hotel's dining highlight.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Western Algarve Day Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Rent a car and drive west to explore the more rugged Algarve coast — the drive from Albufeira through Carvoeiro, Lagos, and out to Sagres takes you through increasingly dramatic and less developed coastal scenery. Stop at the sea caves at Ponta da Piedade near Lagos and finish at the fortress of Sagres on the southwestern tip of Europe for sunset views over the open Atlantic.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Last morning at the Anantara: a morning swim in the pool, then the spa's signature Hammam experience for a final indulgence before departure. The marina promenade for one last coffee, watching the morning yachts depart. Faro airport is 30 minutes away — a short, easy departure from a well-organised honeymoon resort.

caveats: Vilamoura is one of the most developed resort towns in the Algarve — it has excellent infrastructure but limited authentic Portuguese atmosphere. Couples wanting cultural immersion and genuine Portuguese character should explore beyond the resort bubble.

The hotel and resort are popular with golf groups and families in school holidays, which can dilute the romantic atmosphere — the Anantara spa and suite service help mitigate this but cannot fully offset it during peak season.
The Anantara brand brings excellent service standards but also an international homogeneity — if you want a hotel that feels deeply, specifically Portuguese, this is not it.

faqs.question: Is Anantara Vilamoura good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: It is a solid, well-resourced honeymoon resort — the spa is genuinely excellent, the pools are beautiful, Falésia beach is extraordinary, and the service standards are consistently high. It works best for couples who enjoy a full-service international resort with good golf, beach, and spa access. Couples seeking intimacy, Portuguese soul, or a boutique experience will be better served elsewhere.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: May, June, and September are ideal — the Algarve is warm, the sea is swimmable, and Vilamoura is busy but manageable. July and August see the resort at full capacity with golf groups and families; while the facilities are extensive, the atmosphere is at its most resort-like. April and October offer excellent value and pleasant weather for golf and exploring.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Book a suite with butler service early for summer. Reserve spa treatments and any golf tee times before arrival — summer bookings for the Vilamoura Old Course can be in high demand. The Boa Mesa restaurant reservations should also be made in advance for peak season.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, Anantara Vilamoura has a full kids club and actively accommodates families. Vilamoura as a resort area is very family-friendly in summer. The hotel's pools and beach club will have children present in July and August.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: A Deluxe Pool View Suite or Superior Room with terrace gives the best combination of space, comfort, and access to butler services. Suite guests receive personalised Anantara butler assistance that makes the honeymoon more personalised. Request an upper floor room away from the pool for maximum quiet.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Faro Airport (FAO), which is approximately 30km from Vilamoura — about 25 minutes by taxi or private transfer. The hotel can arrange airport transfers. Faro Airport is well served from most European cities and from the US via Lisbon, Madrid, or London.
